pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room, but then it hits me all over again. the house is just... empty. i don't even know why i bother getting up or making coffee or doing anything. it feels like i'm just going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore. everything feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the expansion of the universe and the heat death thing... and it just hit me. it feels so heavy. like, if everything eventually just turns into cold, empty nothingness anyway, what is the point of even trying? it's like we're just running on a treadmill that's eventually going to be deleted. it makes everything feel so incredibly pointless.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room only grows heavier. He is gone. The promises he whispered in the moonlight were nothing but poison, and now I am left with nothing but the hollow ache of his betrayal. It feels as though the very air has become too thick to breathe, a suffocating shroud of memory and regret. I find myself staring out at the black expanse of the moors, thinking how easy it would be to simply... cease. To let the shadows take me, so I might finally find the silence that this cruel world denies me.
